nodes.title: Sustainability Cultural Hst
nodes.description: Through selected readings spanning over two thousand years traces the trajectory of modern notions of ecological and socio-economic sustainability back through time. Includes experiential component at the Instructor's sheep farm. Prerequisites: Three hours in Classics, Environmental Studies, or a related discipline. Cross-listed with: ENVS 2210.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Greco Roman Political Thought
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Greek History/Civilization
nodes.description: Political, social, cultural, and literary development of ancient Greece with assignments appropriate to the introductory level. Students may take CLAS 1320 and CLAS 2320 or equivalent HST courses in different semesters as long as the topics covered are substantially different. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years.

nodes.title: Roman History/Civilization II
nodes.description: Political, social, cultural, and literary development of ancient Rome with assignments appropriate to the intermediate level. Students may take CLAS 1360 and CLAS 2360 or equivalent HST courses in different semesters as long as the topics covered are substantially different. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours in Classics or History.
nodes.other_requirements: Three hours in Classics or History. Open to degree and PACE students. Please email the instructor if you have questions about registration.

nodes.title: TA:Roman History/Civilization
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Classics and Racism in US
nodes.description: Examination of the history of classics in the United States, in particular the relationship between Classics and racism. Examines ancient and modern racial ideologies and histories of slavery, as well as the symbolic power of classics in the antebellum south and today. Prerequisite: Minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at  department discretion.

nodes.title: Roman History/Civilization I
nodes.description: Topics examining Ancient history. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years.

nodes.title: Sustainablity Cultural Hst
nodes.description: Through selected readings spanning over two thousand years traces the trajectory of modern notions of ecological and socio-economic sustainability back through time. Includes experiential component at the Instructor's sheep farm. Prerequisites: Three hours in Environmental Studies, Classics, or a related discipline. Cross-listed with: CLAS 2581.
nodes.other_requirements: Three hours in Environmental Studies, Classics, or a related discipline. Instructor's sheep farm. PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Mythology
nodes.description: Greek myth in literature, art, and music from antiquity to modern times.

nodes.title: Roman History/Civilization II
nodes.description: Topics examining Ancient history. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours in History or Classics.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission.  Students who have taken CLAS 1320, CLAS 2320, HST 1705, HST 2705 in fall 2025 are welcome to register. Please email the instructor if you have questions about registration.

nodes.title: IS:Shakespeare &Clas Antiquity
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Ancient World and Film
nodes.description: Explores structural and thematic connections between selected ancient/classical texts and modern films.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Anc Hist Gaming: Alex theGreat
nodes.description: Topics examining themes in Ancient history. Representative topics: The Peloponesian War; Alexander the Great.  May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years.
